intro: Branch carries medium lock-in (Deep linking, SDK tied but standard) while Adjust is low (Attribution history in proprietary schema; painful to migrate MMPs). Both tools position themselves for mobile, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Branch — Mobile linking and attribution platform with deep linking, deferred deep linking, web-to-app journeys, and MMP capabilities. Adjust — Mobile measurement and analytics platform with deterministic and probabilistic attribution, fraud prevention, and audience segmentation. Branch runs on subscription; Adjust runs on custom. Branch rates 3/5 on transparency versus 1/5 for Adjust. Both tools list mobile apps with paid acquisition as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including mobile apps with paid acquisition. The honest trade-off: Branch is not built for web-only products, and Adjust is not the right call for web-only products, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: Branch is for mobile; Adjust overlaps on the same job; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Branch if mobile and moderate platform coupling is acceptable.
verdict_b: Choose Adjust if mobile and you want low switching cost.

faq.a: The listed pricing for Branch is subscription and for Adjust is custom, which doesn't give a clean apples-to-apples cost. Unit economics and plan structure differ enough that only your real volume answers this. Price both against the same workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Branch to Adjust?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Branch is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Deep linking, SDK tied but standard), and onboarding to Adjust should be straightforward given its low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Branch or Adjust?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, Branch rates 5/5 while Adjust rates 4/5, giving Branch the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is Adjust a good alternative to Branch?
faq.a: Yes, Adjust targets the same job Branch does — mobile — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (custom vs subscription) and lock-in (low lock-in (Attribution history in proprietary schema; painful to migrate MMPs) vs medium lock-in (Deep linking, SDK tied but standard)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Adjust is explicitly not for web-only products.
